title: STIAS Individual Fellowship
description: The Stellenbosch Institute for Advanced Study (STIAS) hosts scholars, scientists, and artists in Stellenbosch, South Africa for independent research residencies of six weeks to five months across two annual semesters. Fellows get a furnished residential stay, office/studio access, and join a full program of seminars and public lectures; no restriction is placed on nationality, discipline, or academic affiliation.
visaNote: South African research/visitor visa required for extended stays; STIAS assists with arrangements.

title: Kuona Trust Sponsored Artist Residency
description: Kuona Trust's flagship sponsored residency in Nairobi supports two visual artists a year with studio space, a stipend, a travel allowance, and housing for one to two months, culminating in a mandatory public exhibition. Kuona is one of East Africa's longest-running contemporary art centres.
org: Kuona Trust Centre for Visual Arts
visaNote: Standard Kenyan tourist e-visa is generally sufficient for the residency period; confirm with Kuona before travel.

title: dot.ateliers Artist Residency
description: dot.ateliers, founded by artist Amoako Boafo, runs an annual open-call studio residency in Accra, Ghana for artists 18 and older of any nationality or background. The programme provides studio space, housing, and a materials stipend, with additional travel support for artists based within Africa, ending in a year-end group exhibition.
visaNote: Ghanaian entry visa requirements vary by nationality; travel support is only available to artists based within Africa.

title: CCA Tashkent Artist and Researcher Residencies
description: The Centre for Contemporary Art (CCA) Tashkent runs an eight-week residency three times a year at restored historic mahalla sites in Tashkent, hosting international artists alongside curators, writers, and researchers, plus one Uzbek practitioner per cohort. The programme covers travel, visa, and accommodation costs, plus a scholarship and materials budget.
org: Centre for Contemporary Art Tashkent (Uzbekistan Art and Culture Development Foundation)
visaNote: Uzbekistan visa for the residency period is arranged and covered by CCA Tashkent.

title: Masaha Residency
description: Misk Art Institute's Masaha Residency is a three-month, in-house visual arts residency in Riyadh for twelve emerging artists per cycle, open to any nationality with at least three years of practice or exhibiting history. It fully funds a round-trip flight, Saudi visa, four-star hotel accommodation, breakfast and dinner, daily transport, studio space, mentorship, and a production budget.
visaNote: Saudi entry visa for the residency period is fully arranged and covered by Misk Art Institute.

title: Sharjah Art Foundation Residency Programme
description: Sharjah Art Foundation's Residency Programme hosts visual artists, writers, researchers, musicians, and performance artists at Bait Obaid Al Shamsi in Sharjah City or the Kalba Ice Factory. It covers flights and transportation to Sharjah, accommodation, and a monthly stipend, with access to the Foundation's institutional network.
visaNote: UAE entry/residency permit for the residency period is arranged by Sharjah Art Foundation as needed.

title: Sangam House Writers' Residency
description: Sangam House is an international writers' residency in India (the 2026-27 season is based in Bangalore) offering one-month stays to writers of any nationality and language, including a required letter of recommendation and 50-page writing sample. It covers board and lodging in a shared residency setting; writers arrange and fund their own travel.
visaNote: Standard Indian tourist or business visa required; not arranged by Sangam House.

title: Vasl International Artists' Residency
description: Vasl Artists' Association, part of the global Triangle Network, hosts an international artists' residency in Karachi connecting visiting and local artists through studio access, mentorship from established professionals, and structured engagement with the city's contemporary art scene.
visaNote: Pakistani visa required; type and sponsorship vary by residency track — confirm with Vasl before applying.

title: MMCA International Artist Residency (Changdong/Goyang)
description: The National Museum of Modern and Contemporary Art, Korea (MMCA) runs studio residency terms at its Changdong and Goyang facilities near Seoul for Korean and international visual artists, with each term running roughly two to three months. The international artist track includes a monthly stipend of approximately KRW 1,000,000 alongside studio space and exhibition opportunities.
org: National Museum of Modern and Contemporary Art, Korea (MMCA)
visaNote: South Korean cultural/artist visa sponsored with MMCA support required for the residency period.

title: Jan Michalski Foundation Residency for Writers
description: The Jan Michalski Foundation hosts writers and translators (and other literature-centred practitioners) for two-week to three-month residencies at its estate in Montricher, Switzerland. It covers round-trip travel to and from the residency, a weekly allowance of CHF 400, and breakfast and lunch; applications for the 2027 residency window are open from 2 June through 31 August 2026.
org: Fondation Jan Michalski pour l'écriture et la littérature
visaNote: Schengen short-stay visa may be required depending on nationality; this is not a work visa.

title: Solitude Fellowship
description: Akademie Schloss Solitude in Stuttgart awards 50-60 residential fellowships every two years to international artists, researchers, and cultural professionals across visual, aural, spatial, textual, digital, and scientific disciplines, for six- or nine-month stays. It includes a monthly stipend of EUR 1,300, a furnished live/work studio, travel reimbursement, and health insurance for non-EU fellows.
visaNote: German long-stay residence permit required for non-EU fellows; the Akademie supports the application and provides health insurance.

title: Art Explora - Cité Internationale des Arts Residency
description: The Art Explora residency programme at Cité internationale des arts gives international artists a furnished studio-apartment in Paris, a monthly living grant of EUR 1,000, a production grant of up to EUR 3,000, and round-trip travel, for a multi-month period of research and creation.
org: Cité internationale des arts / Art Explora
visaNote: French long-stay visa (visa de long séjour) required for non-EU residents.

title: Delfina Foundation Residency Programme
description: Delfina Foundation runs a rotating series of fully funded thematic and regional residencies at its London base, each typically twelve weeks long. Recent cycles have focused on artists based in Southeast Asia, East Africa, and Latin America and the Caribbean; funding covers travel, accommodation at the Delfina house, and a daily living allowance.
visaNote: UK Standard Visitor visa is typically sufficient; Delfina provides supporting documentation.

title: Sacatar Institute Fellowship
description: Instituto Sacatar hosts an eight-week interdisciplinary artist residency on the island of Itaparica near Salvador, Bahia, Brazil, providing a private room, a separate studio, meals, laundry, and logistical support. Artists cover their own airfare, except for one Global South awardee whose round-trip flight is funded annually through a partner grant.
visaNote: Brazilian entry requirements vary by nationality; Sacatar does not sponsor visas.

title: Fundación Casa Wabi x ArtReview Residency Prize
description: Casa Wabi's annual residency prize, run with ArtReview, brings an artist to its Tadao Ando-designed campus in Puerto Escondido, Oaxaca, Mexico for five to six weeks, requiring a reciprocal community-engagement project. It covers a domestic flight from Mexico City, lodging, all meals, and materials/project support.
visaNote: Mexican tourist entry sufficient for most nationalities; international travel to Mexico City is self-funded.

title: Malba Writers' Residency (REM)
description: MALBA's Residencia de Escritores (REM) hosts published foreign writers in Buenos Aires for a five-week stay combining manuscript work with public literary events and engagement with the city's cultural scene. Applicants must have published at least three books (novel, short story, poetry, children's literature, or literary non-fiction); the programme covers board and lodging.
org: Museo de Arte Latinoamericano de Buenos Aires (MALBA)
visaNote: Argentine tourist entry sufficient for most nationalities; travel costs are the writer's responsibility.

title: VAC Residency Hanoi
description: VAC Residency, run by the non-profit Vietnam Art Collection in Hanoi's Tay Ho district, offers four three-month terms a year to two artists based in Vietnam at a time, combining project development with a final month of open studio. Collaborative cycles run with the AiRViNe network have included a stipend and production budget.

title: Desert 23°S Residency
description: La Wayaka Current's Desert 23°S residency brings an international, multidisciplinary group of artists and researchers to Chile's Atacama Desert for site-based work exploring aridity and geology. The programme offers financial aid and a limited number of partial and fully funded places each year, prioritizing artists from Latin America, the Caribbean, and BIPOC applicants; other participants contribute to programme costs.
visaNote: Chilean tourist entry sufficient for most nationalities.
